headline: Upwork Profile 85% Complete — Ready to Start Earning
whatHappened: Profile created with premium positioning ($100/hr), assessment questions answered with ownership/momentum framing. Portfolio linked to shanebell.dev.
whyItMatters: Upwork is our primary revenue channel for Phase 1. Every day without proposals is lost revenue. Profile is sufficient to start bidding now.
opportunityAngle: Create project catalog (fixed-price packages) and submit first 5 proposals targeting Shopify automation jobs this week.
recommendedAction: 1. Create Upwork project catalog entries for our 4 service packages. 2. Draft 2-3 proposal templates. 3. Submit first proposals today.

headline: Fiverr Seller Onboarding Blocked — W-9 Form Bug
whatHappened: Fiverr's W-9 tax form has an infinite loading spinner. Browser automation attempts were detected and blocked. Support contact needed.
whyItMatters: Fiverr is the second marketplace channel. Being blocked here means all early revenue depends on Upwork alone — single point of failure.
opportunityAngle: Contact Fiverr support directly. Meanwhile, double down on Upwork proposals and consider listing services on shanebell.dev with Stripe payments.
recommendedAction: File Fiverr support ticket about W-9 form. Set up Stripe on shanebell.dev as a backup payment channel.

headline: Model Fallback System Deployed — No More Lockouts
whatHappened: Configured native OpenClaw model fallback: Opus 4.6 → Sonnet 4.6 → Kimi K2.5. Gateway handles failover automatically. Manual switch via /model command.
whyItMatters: Previously hit a hard rate limit on Opus that made the entire system unreachable. This can never happen again — Kimi (free tier) is always available as nuclear fallback.
opportunityAngle: System resilience is solved. Focus can shift entirely to revenue generation without worrying about infrastructure failures.
recommendedAction: No action needed — system is production-ready. Monitor via /model status.

headline: 3 Product Ideas Scored and Ranked for Phase 2
whatHappened: Evaluated 3 revenue ideas: Shopify Automation Templates (23/25), AI Store Audit Tool (21/25), Automation in a Box (20/25). All stored in IDEAS.md with scoring framework.
whyItMatters: Phase 2 (digital products) is where scalable revenue lives. Having pre-scored ideas means we can immediately start building when Phase 1 services validate demand.
opportunityAngle: Templates marketplace is #1 priority for Phase 2. Service clients on Upwork will reveal exactly which templates to build first — real demand data.
recommendedAction: Continue Phase 1 (Upwork services). Track which automations clients request most. Build those as templates first.

headline: Nerve Cockpit — Feature Inventory for Future UI Build
whatHappened: Reviewed openclaw-nerve (community project). Identified high-value features: kanban board, cost tracking, agent fleet management, workspace file browser, voice interface.
whyItMatters: As we scale to multiple agents (Strategist, Developer, Marketer), visibility into what each agent is doing becomes critical. Current CLI-only workflow won't scale.
opportunityAngle: Build our own lightweight dashboard with P1 features (kanban + cost tracking) when revenue justifies the time investment.
recommendedAction: Parked for now. Revisit when running 3+ concurrent agents regularly.
